title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues in Boston, showcasing songs from their latest album released last year.
The band engaged the audience with energetic performances, including songs like 'Tick Tick Boom' and 'Countdown To Shutdown.'
Australian band The Chats opened the concert, delivering an energetic garage rock set prior to The Hives' performance.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, a 42-year-old from Braintree, is running her third Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
After a Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosis in 2023, McHugh overcame treatment challenges to resume running through physical therapy.
McHugh honors her late mentor and runs to demonstrate resilience and support cancer-related causes.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and colleagues question Meta's facial recognition plans in smart glasses, citing privacy concerns.
A letter from senators urges Meta to clarify data handling and opt-out options for facial recognition technology.
The inquiry arrives before March 28 protests, as senators warn of potential misuse at rallies and public events.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed vote on $2.65 million for new PFAS-free firefighter gear.
Current gear contains PFAS, linked to cancer; firefighters pushed for urgent funding approval.
Proposal for new gear includes two sets per firefighter to reduce long-term exposure risks.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro officials propose turning Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve to address $57 million in necessary repairs.
The transition would focus on native wildlife and conservation, with reduced or free public admission on select weekends.
Capron Park Zoo opened in 1937, housing over 100 animals; a decision-making team is to be formed by July.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Colin Wong and Abigail Brown had a last-minute microwedding at Bow Market due to a blizzard disrupting their plans.
Kara Baskin reflects on 1990s communication through FX's show about John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's romance.
Love Letters podcast explores the implications and usage of the term 'partner' in modern relationships.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, a Burlington resident, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his late father and his own cancer journey.
Averback, 44, began running after losing his father to glioblastoma and surviving testicular cancer, using it as therapy and structure.
His journey with Team MGH was challenged by a serious blood infection, but he remains committed to completing the marathon.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Philadelphia Eagles WR A.J. Brown and Patriots S Kevin Byard are training together amidst trade rumors.
The Patriots reportedly offered a deal for Brown, but the Eagles aim to wait until June post-June 1 moves.
The Eagles seek a high price for Brown, with comparisons drawn to Jaylen Waddle's recent trade value.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens concluded his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
Boston Bruins consider signing prospect James Hagens after Boston College's season ended in the Hockey East semifinals.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney suggests Hagens is a potential addition for late-season games following the team's quiet trade deadline.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' on Netflix follows Tommy Shelby facing Nazi threats in 1940s Birmingham.
'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett as a stand-up comedian is streaming on Disney+.
'Born to Bowl', a docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is now available on HBO Max.
